items.title: Cricket's next generation of pace knows fast bowling can take a toll

children.content: Cricket has few more glorious sights than a fast bowler terrorising a top-class batting order, but becoming a speedster is not without its pains, as the nation's next generation is finding out.

gallery.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

thumbnail.altText: Simrth Gill tosses up a cricket ball, while holding a cricket bat.

items.title: Factory workers reunite 16 years after closure of major food manufacturer

children.content: Sixteen years after workers clocked off for the final time, Berrivale workers reunited to remember the cannery and preserving factory that employed generations of locals.

image.altText: About a dozen women working on a production line in a factory.

items.title: 'We're taking him home': Aboriginal stockman's remains repatriated

children.content: An Aboriginal stockman whose remains have been held by the South Australian Museum for six decades will this week be returned to Country in a remote national park.

image.altText: The start of a repatriation process at the SA Museum.

items.title: Truro businesses call for certainty on freight bypass project

children.content: SA businesses situated along a major thoroughfare say they are holding back on making decisions about their future, amid uncertainty surrounding a slated road project that could divert traffic around the area.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

items.title: Travis Head and wife Jess cop social media abuse after IPL on-field incident

children.content: Australian cricketer Travis Head and his wife Jess Head have their Instagram accounts blasted with vile comments after a series of tense on-field exchanges between Head and Indian star Virat Kohli in an Indian Premier League match.

image.altText: Australian batsman Travis head looks at his bat while Indian fielder Virat Kohli smiles and shouts.

items.title: Dog-friendly cafe issued warning after customer complaint

children.content: Kellie Hunter opened her cafe in the Adelaide Hills in 2023 as a friendly place for dogs and their owners, but the local council has now put a stop to it after one complaint.

gallery.altText: A woman speaking to a dog standing on its back legs at a cafe serving window

thumbnail.altText: A woman speaking to a dog standing on its back legs at a cafe serving window

items.title: Amid push to rename Grey, SA's largest federal seat, who was its namesake?

children.content: Sir George Grey was the former governor of South Australia and is remembered as a "bundle of contradictions" in both Australia and New Zealand, where he played a key role in the Māori wars. But amid calls for the seat that bears his name to be renamed, who was Grey?

image.altText: A collage showing a historic portrait photo in black and white with a map of the seat of Grey and a painting from the NZ wars

items.title: Government bails out Rex's multi-million-dollar debt to regional airports

children.content: Council-owned airports have been bailed out by the federal government to the tune of $4.8 million, $2 million of which was for NSW alone.

image.altText: The outside of an aircraft printed with the letters REX in blue and orange

items.title: Australian astronaut offered place on breakthrough space flight

children.content: The European Space Agency has formally extended the offer for Australia's Katherine Bennell-Pegg to join a mission that would take her to the International Space Station, but the place is not guaranteed.

gallery.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.

thumbnail.altText: An astronaut with a globe of the earth.

items.title: Vegemite banned, Tim Tams welcome: Baseballer brings Aussie flavour to US

children.content: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

image.altText: Curtis Mead on the field during a baseball game.

items.title: Gold medallist who endured 16 surgeries in 9 months hangs up oars

children.content: Paralympic gold medallist Nikki Ayers reflects on a career that saw her face ableism, homophobia and sexism.

image.altText: woman holds a golf medal

items.title: Autumn rain in SA brings 'incredible' boom in birdlife

children.content: Parts of South Australia are preparing for an exceptional year as autumn rain brings an increase in birdlife.

image.altText: A pelican swims in a river and is reflected in the water.

items.title: 'Midwits' and media bust-ups herald One Nation's arrival in SA parliament

children.content: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie look on at a woman wearing dark top, the man is also seen in a camera viewfinder

items.title: Plagued by 18 months of blackouts, locals say $10m fix is long overdue

children.content: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.

articles.title: What we know about the remaining families returning from Syria

children.content: The last cohort of ISIS-linked Australians living in a notorious Syrian camp are expected to return back into Australia in the coming hours. Here's what we know about them.

items.title: Bringing Them Home inaction a 'woeful failure', ex-commissioner says

items.title: What do plummeting clearance rates mean for the property market?

children.content: Early signs of an Australian housing market correction are now clearly visible, and at the heart of the recent property market weakness is less vendor power.

items.title: After losing her 3yo daughter, one mum says MDMA therapy saved her life

image.altText: Woman with short brown hair looks at camera in a green silk shirt and in a well lit therapy room.

children.content: Jess Yugovich is among the Australians trying MDMA-assisted psychotherapy after being diagnosed with PTSD, as highly regulated facilities process the drug for therapeutic use.

items.title: Witness recounts 'catastrophic failure' of Vivid Sydney drone show

items.title: One world record and a whole lot of regret for Enhanced Games athletes

children.content: Despite the drugs and the super suits, the Enhanced Games delivered little more than a smattering of PBs and a single world fastest time for the hefty price of an athlete's hard-earned credibility.

items.title: 'My time to go': The words that showed Daniher's humanity in tragedy

items.title: Australian family demands action one year after son's Bali death

items.title: Flexible work broader than just working from home

children.content: A bill amending the Fair Work Act 2009 to include the right to work from home is too narrow in its focus and could lead to discrimination, according to submissions in a federal inquiry.
